title: Registry Identity, SBOM, Provenance, Attestations, and Promotion · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Github Artifact Attest Action
summary: A release promotes a mutable tag, rebuilds for production, stores an SBOM separately from the image, generates provenance with leaked build args, and never verifies attestations downstream. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci github artifact attest action increment in attested image release record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use actions/attest with least permissions and exact subject path or digest while recognizing plan, repository visibility, and registry constraints.
Generate image SBOM and max-appropriate provenance, identify builder, source, inputs, subject digest, limits, and registry attachment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example candidate is promoted by mutable tag with partial SBOM and provenance, an unverified attestation, and policy exception 3. Bind image index, platforms, signatures, attestations, verification policy, and exception expiry. Competency probe: Use actions/attest with least permissions and exact subject path or digest while recognizing plan, repository visibility, and registry constraints. A defensible solution applies Ci Github Artifact Attest Action, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to attested image release record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-github-artifact-attest-action — Use actions/attest with least permissions and exact subject path or digest while recognizing plan, repository visibility, and registry constraints.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-image-release-attestations-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-image-release-attestations-3
    artifact: attested-release.yaml
    decision: worked ci image release attestations 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      subject: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      tags: [v2.4.0, stable]
      sbom: spdx-attached
      provenance: mode-max
      builder: github-hosted-reviewed-workflow
      actions-attest: 4
      attestation-subject: exact-digest
      verification: issuer-repository-workflow-builder-subject
      promotion: same-digest
      rollback: prior-verified-digest
    verify: verify image digest SBOM provenance signature issuer repository workflow builder subject predicate policy and promotion identity
    failure: Reject tags as identity, detached unbound SBOM, build-arg secrets, signature-only trust, unverified attestations, or transitive SLSA claims.
    repair: Bind metadata to digest, generate safe attestations, verify identity and expectations downstream, promote once, and state SLSA limits.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
